trends.title: Cursor (Anysphere) Exploring $5B Raise at Up to $60B Valuation
trends.summary: Newcomer reports that Cursor-maker Anysphere is exploring a $5B fundraise at a valuation of up to $60B. This would make it one of the largest AI raises ever and puts it in the same valuation tier as Anthropic.
trends.why: A coding assistant reaching $60B valuation validates that AI-native developer tools are being priced as platform companies, not features. It also signals that the 'AI application layer' can command frontier-lab-tier valuations when distribution is strong enough.
tweets:
cursor (anysphere) reportedly exploring a $5B raise at up to $60B valuation per @EricNewcomer.

for context:

1. that's anthropic-tier valuation for a coding assistant
2. cursor launched ~2 years ago
3. this would be one of the largest AI raises ever

what's interesting: AI application layer companies can now command the same valuations as frontier labs -- if distribution is strong enough. question is where the ceiling is (h't @EricNewcomer)

fyi cursor's parent anysphere is weighing a $5B raise at up to $60B.

for context, anthropic just raised at $60B too. but anthropic is a frontier lab training models. cursor is a coding tool built on top of those models.

the implied thesis: owning the developer workflow is as valuable as owning the model. that's a big bet on the application layer

trends.title: CFTC Launches Event-Contract Rulemaking Amid Conflicting Kalshi Court Rulings
trends.summary: The CFTC is moving toward formal rulemaking on event contracts, including integrity and manipulation guidance, after an Ohio judge ruled Kalshi's sports contracts constitute sports betting. This comes alongside reports of Kalshi and Polymarket targeting $20B valuations.
trends.why: Formal CFTC rulemaking could either legitimize prediction markets with clear rules or restrict them with onerous compliance. The outcome shapes whether $20B valuations hold — and whether prediction markets become regulated infrastructure or face a fragmented state-by-state reality.

sources.label: Marketplace — CFTC Considers Regulating Prediction Markets

tweets:
the CFTC is now moving toward formal rulemaking on event contracts per @ESPN and @Marketplace.

this comes after:

1. ohio judge ruled kalshi sports contracts = sports betting
2. conflicting court decisions across jurisdictions
3. kalshi + polymarket both targeting ~$20B valuations

the outcome matters: clear federal rules could legitimize the space. state-by-state fragmentation could slow it down for years. this is the regulatory moment for prediction markets

fyi CFTC is kicking off event-contract rulemaking -- integrity guidance, manipulation rules, the works.

for context, prediction markets are in a weird spot: $20B valuations on one hand, an ohio judge calling sports contracts 'sports betting' on the other.

formal rulemaking is how this gets resolved one way or another. watch for comment periods and draft rules over the next 6-12 months

trends.title: Stablecoin Market Cap Adds $3B in a Week — USDC Supply Hits $81.1B
trends.summary: DefiLlama data shows stablecoin market cap grew +$3.073B in the past week. Artemis shows USDC supply at $81.1B, continuing its steady climb. Total stablecoin market cap now exceeds $316B.
trends.why: Consistent weekly supply expansion is the clearest signal of capital entering the onchain economy. USDC at $81B+ with steady growth suggests institutional flows are sustained, not episodic — important for anyone modeling stablecoin adoption curves.
tweets:
stablecoin market cap added $3.07B in the past week per @DefiLlama. USDC supply now at $81.1B per artemis.

1. total stablecoin mcap: $316B+
2. USDC growing steadily -- not episodic spikes, sustained weekly expansion
3. this on top of $1.8T monthly transfer volume ATH

consistent supply growth = capital entering the onchain economy at a steady pace. that's the adoption signal to track, not price action

trends.title: White House Floating Rare Intermeeting Fed Cut — Last One Was March 2020
trends.summary: Nick Timiraos flagged that the president is pushing for a rare intermeeting Fed rate cut, with the last such move on March 15, 2020 during the COVID shock. The next scheduled decision is in ~5 business days and markets expect a hold.
trends.why: Intermeeting cuts are reserved for acute market stress — even the rhetoric can move rate vol and front-end yields. The question is whether this stays political noise or starts shifting the reaction function.
tweets:
the white house is now floating an intermeeting fed cut.

for context, the last one was march 15, 2020 -- a stress regime tool.

1. next scheduled decision is in ~5 business days
2. markets currently pricing 'no change'
3. even talking about it can move rate vol + 2Y yields

what would have to break for the fed to actually go early? (h't @NickTimiraos)

fyi timiraos is flagging intermeeting cut chatter out of the white house. last one was mar 2020.

maybe it's just politics. but if front-end yields + rate vol start moving on it, that's your tell (per @NickTimiraos)
